Missing-data treatment: Uses the harmonized dataset after conservative interpolation or justified imputation
Variables included: HealthExp_GDP, Pop65, Dependency, GDP_pc, Gov_Share, LTC_Workers, and derived indicators where available
Purpose of robustness check: Provides the benchmark specification for descriptive, econometric, and machine-learning analysis
Expected interpretation: Establishes the broadest country-year reference model while retaining caution for high-missingness variables

Missing-data treatment: Excludes observations with missing values in the variables required for each model
Variables included: Same model structure as R1, but only complete observations are retained
Purpose of robustness check: Tests whether results depend on imputed values
Expected interpretation: If coefficient signs, feature rankings, and model performance remain similar, the findings are robust to missing-data treatment

Missing-data treatment: Retains only variables with strong country-year coverage
Purpose of robustness check: Tests the stability of demographic and macroeconomic effects without high-missingness financing and workforce variables
Expected interpretation: Confirms whether aging, dependency pressure, and macroeconomic capacity remain central drivers of health expenditure burden

Missing-data treatment: Adds government/public financing share where available or conservatively imputed
Variables included: HealthExp_GDP, Pop65, Dependency, GDP_pc, Gov_Share, Public_Exposure, FPI
Purpose of robustness check: Tests whether public financing structure changes the interpretation of aging-related fiscal pressure
Expected interpretation: Shows whether public financing exposure provides additional policy insight, while remaining limited by Gov_Share coverage

Missing-data treatment: Adds long-term care workforce variables where data are available
Variables included: HealthExp_GDP, Pop65, Dependency, GDP_pc, LTC_Workers, Worker_Ratio
Purpose of robustness check: Tests whether formal care workforce capacity improves model interpretation or predictive performance
Expected interpretation: Indicates whether workforce availability acts as a service-capacity dimension, while remaining limited by workforce-data coverage

Purpose of robustness check: Tests whether results are driven by mature health and long-term care systems
Expected interpretation: Supports interpretation of aging-related financing pressure in advanced economies

Purpose of robustness check: Tests whether results differ in less mature health and long-term care systems
Expected interpretation: Supports exploratory interpretation of aging-related financing pressure in emerging economies

Purpose of robustness check: Tests whether conclusions depend on the selected sustainability indicator
Expected interpretation: If results remain directionally consistent, the sustainability framework is strengthened across alternative aging-related financing indicators
